subject: math
title: Decimals and Place Value
content:
Decimals and Place Value

What are Decimals?

Decimals are another way to write fractions, especially when parts of a whole are less than 1. They use a decimal point (.) to separate the whole number from the fractional part.

Understanding Place Value

Let's look at the number 47.823:

• 4 in the tens place = 40
• 7 in the ones place = 7
• 8 in the tenths place = 0.8
• 2 in the hundredths place = 0.02
• 3 in the thousandths place = 0.003

Reading Decimals

To read a decimal, read the whole number, then the decimal point as 'and', then the fractional part as a whole number with the last place value.

Examples:
• 2.5 = "two and five-tenths"
• 3.47 = "three and forty-seven hundredths"
• 12.803 = "twelve and eight hundred three thousandths"

Converting Fractions to Decimals

Divide the numerator by the denominator:
• 1/2 = 1 ÷ 2 = 0.5
• 3/4 = 3 ÷ 4 = 0.75
• 1/8 = 1 ÷ 8 = 0.125

Comparing Decimals

To compare decimals, line them up by the decimal point and compare digit by digit from left to right.

Example: Which is larger, 2.34 or 2.41?
• Both have 2 in the ones place
• Compare tenths: 3 vs 4
• Since 4 > 3, 2.41 > 2.34

questions.prompt: In the number 5.67, what digit is in the tenths place?
questions.hint: The first digit after the decimal point is tenths

questions.prompt: What is 1/4 written as a decimal?

questions.hint: Compare tenths place after the decimal

questions.prompt: How do you write 'twelve and three tenths' as a decimal?
questions.hint: 'Twelve' is whole, 'three tenths' is the decimal part

questions.prompt: What is 0.75 as a fraction in simplest form?
